mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate below 9.0% signals improving labor market conditions, boosting corporate earnings visibility and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in periods with UNRATE < 9.0% fall below 0.5% on average, or the correlation between UNRATE and SPX returns drops below 0.25.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in industrial production above its 3-month moving average indicates strengthening manufacturing activity, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in periods with INDPRO above MA fall below 2.0% on average, or the predictive power of INDPRO drops below 0.15 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent rise in housing starts (HOUST) and real sales (RSAFS) signals robust consumer demand, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in periods with both HOUST and RSAFS growth fail to exceed 1.5% on average, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly contraction in GDP (GDPC1) combined with rising housing starts (HOUST) indicates a stagflationary risk, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 decline > 1% QoQ and HOUST rise > 4% YoY
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in periods with GDPC1 contraction and HOUST growth exceed 2.0% on average, or the interaction term's sign reverses.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in CPI (CPIAUCSL) above 2.5% signals inflationary pressures, prompting tighter monetary policy and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in periods with CPIAUCSL > 2.5% exceed 1.0% on average, or the correlation between CPI and SPX returns falls below -0.3.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) above 100k monthly indicates strong labor market health, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in periods with PAYEMS > 100k fail to exceed 1.0% on average, or the predictive power of PAYEMS drops below 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in consumer confidence (RSAFS) below 350 billion indicates weakening demand, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in periods with RSAFS < 350B exceed 0.5% on average, or the correlation between RSAFS and SPX returns drops below -0.2.
